Title: Commercial Senior Planner
Location: Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam, Lô E2, Đường số 1, KCN Tân Đô, Đức Hòa Hạ, Đức Hòa, Long An
Job Description:
Position: Commercial Senior Planner
Department: Commercial Operations
Report to: Commercial Operations Manager and General Director

1. Job Purpose:
The Commercial Senior Planner is responsible for leading demand planning and order planning processes for the Commercial team.

This role serves as the key coordination point between Sales, Business Development, and Factory Planning to ensure accurate forecasting, efficient production alignment, and smooth order execution.

By consolidating commercial demand and coordinating closely with factory planners, the role ensures better material forecasting, improved production follow-up, and reliable delivery commitments to customers.

2. Key Responsibilities:
- Demand Planning & Forecast Management:
• Lead the demand planning process by consolidating sales forecasts from Sales and Business Development teams.
• Analyze demand trends, order patterns, and sales pipelines to improve forecast accuracy.
• Translate commercial demand into structured planning inputs for factory planning teams.
• Work closely with factory planners to ensure material readiness and production capacity alignment.

- Commercial Order Planning:
• Oversee order planning flow from order confirmation to production scheduling.
• Ensure orders are aligned with production plans and committed delivery timelines.
• Identify potential risks in capacity, materials, or lead time and propose mitigation actions.

- Cross-functional Coordination:
• Act as the key coordination point between Commercial teams (Sales & Business Development) and Factory Planning.
• Align production priorities with commercial commitments and market demand.
• Support Sales teams with planning insights related to lead time, order feasibility, and delivery schedules.

- Production Monitoring & Order Follow-up:
• Monitor production status of key orders and proactively follow up with factory planners when necessary.
• Ensure timely communication of production progress and delivery updates to commercial teams.
• Escalate production risks or delays when required.

- Planning Process Improvement:
• Identify gaps in current planning workflows between Commercial and Factory teams.
• Propose and implement improvements to enhance planning efficiency and coordination.
• Develop planning tools, reports, and dashboards to support decision-making.

- Reporting & Analysis:
• Prepare demand planning reports, order tracking reports, and planning performance dashboards.
• Provide planning insights and analysis to support Commercial management in operational and strategic decisions.
3. Decision Influence & Authority:
• Provide planning recommendations to prioritize production orders based on commercial demand, customer importance, and delivery commitments.
• Support Sales and Business Development teams with planning inputs for lead time commitments, order feasibility, and delivery strategies.
• Proactively identify potential supply, capacity, or material constraints and recommend planning adjustments to minimize delivery risks.
• Collaborate with Factory Planning to align production priorities with commercial objectives and customer requirements.
• Contribute to planning decisions that impact order scheduling, delivery timelines, and demand allocation.
4. Key Performance Indicators (KPIs):
• Forecast Accuracy
• Order Fulfillment Rate
• On-time Delivery Performance
• Production Alignment with Commercial Demand
• Planning Efficiency and Responsiveness

Title: Production Supervisor Leader
Location: 36 VSIP, Street No. 4, Vietnam – Singapore Industrial Park, Binh Hoa Ward, Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam, Đường Trần Phú, KCN Nhơn Trạch I, Xã Nhơn Trạch, Tỉnh Đồng Nai.
Job Description:
Company Overview:
Coherent Corp. is a global leaders in datacenter and communications industries. Headquartered in Saxonburg, Pennsylvania, USA, and founded in 1971, Coherent operates in over 20 countries worldwide. Since entering Vietnam in 2005, Coherent has expanded to over 2,400 employees across Binh Duong (VSIP1), Dong Nai (Nhon Trach 1), Hanoi, and Bac Ninh (Yen Phong IP). As part of our global growth strategy and commitment to local talent development, we aim to create over 4,000 new job opportunities in Vietnam within the next five years.
Work Location: VSIP 1 - Binh Duong
BASIC FUNCTION OF THE JOB:
Responsible for production and documentation, LEAN manufacturing, method analysis/ improvements, machines layout and equipment acquisition, preparation of equipment justification and appropriation request development, statistical process control systems, providing analytical troubleshooting leadership, hands-on to involve process and production activity is must.

People Management
1) Develop staff competency & training plan.
2) Training/ coaching, OJT, quality staff to ensure enough capability.
3) Staff performance setting & evaluation
4) Routine communicates to all staff performance and company policy, requirement.
5) Establish the SOP, WI and other documentation as required.

Cost Saving
1) Improvement Project for cost saving and capability improvement.
2) Develop alternative material, tooling, consumable, machine.
3) optimizing process to shorten cycle time.

Production Management
1) Daily planning resource and control to meet OTD, Yield, Productivity and labour cost
2) Daily cooperate to other function ENG, PPM, PIC, QA to resolve daily issue.
3) Ensure all production standards control plan is compiled as Quality, EHS …
4) Be able to use analytical trouble -shouting techniques to solve problems.
5) Lead and/or participates in resolutions or problem (4M1E)
6) Lead the team to join the training and qualification for new products transferring if needed.
7) Contribute to the planning and execution of department objectives

Production Support
1) Support new product acquisition.
2) 5S improvement in workplace.
3) Work with others department to implement the company’s policies and goals.

Title: [HCM] New Store Development Planning Specialist
Location: 1st Floor, Topaz Building, Sai Gon Pearl, 92 Nguyen Huu Canh, Binh Thanh District, 92 Nguyen Huu Canh Street, Ward 22, Binh Thanh District, Ho Chi Minh City
Job Description:
Company Description
4P’s, headquartered in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam, operates 40 worldwide restaurants. Our main restaurant, Pizza 4P’s, has 35 stores in Vietnam and manages global franchises like IPPUDO and ABOUT LIFE COFFEE BREWERS. Our core products are food and beverages, specifically pizza, with key differentiators of "House-made cheeses", "Earth-to-people" and "Omotenashi" hospitality.

Pizza 4P’s is an innovative Vietnam-based Japanese Italian pizza restaurant that started in Vietnam with a simple yet powerful mission: “Delivering Wow, Sharing Happiness”. We are known for crafting artisanal pizzas, homemade cheese, and unique East-meets-West flavors while embracing sustainability and community impact at the heart of everything we do. Today, we operate restaurants across Vietnam, Cambodia, Japan, India and Indonesia, and continue to grow with a vision of bringing people together through memorable dining experiences.

Job Description

I. ROLE PURPOSE

The New Store Development Planning Specialist is responsible for overseeing on-site construction activities to ensure projects are delivered on time, within budget, and to the highest quality and safety standards. This role requires close coordination with contractors, subcontractors, and internal teams to maintain compliance with approved designs, safety regulations, and company standards.

II. KEY ACCOUNTABILITIES

1. Location Hunting Strategy

Utilize data analytics and market research tools to pinpoint potential growth markets and high-traffic areas.

Collaborate with marketing and sales teams to align location strategies with business objectives.

Leverage industry contacts and networking to stay informed about emerging site leads.

Refine location selection criteria based on performance metrics and market dynamics.

2. Lease Negotiation and Lease Contract Review

Conduct detailed financial analysis and lease comparisons to evaluate economic impact.

Lead negotiations and financial evaluations to secure favorable terms for new restaurant locations.

Review and analyze lease contracts for compliance with company standards and legal requirements.

Collaborate with Legal and Finance teams to resolve disputes and mitigate risks.

3. Compliance, Contract, and Payment Documentation

Maintain accurate records of planning activities and obtain necessary permits.

Ensure all administrative and legal documentation is accurate, timely, and compliant.

Verify project-related contracts (construction, installation, etc.) for accuracy.

Check contractor and supplier payment documentation for compliance with contract terms.

Process and follow up on payment requests with internal and external parties.

4. Design & Construction Collaboration

Work closely with architects and designers to develop restaurant layouts that meet brand standards and customer expectations.

Coordinate with technical teams to ensure brand alignment in store design.

5. Timeline & Budget Management

Track project timelines and address potential delays or bottlenecks.

Ensure project efficiency through rigorous monitoring and stakeholder communication.

Communicate project status, milestones, and roadblocks to executive leadership.

Forecast and track costs across all aspects of the planning process.

6. Data Analysis & Reporting

Analyze new store performance data to identify areas for improvement.

Provide insights and transparency through data-driven reporting.

Prepare reports on project status, risks, and opportunities using project management software.

7. Risk Management

Develop contingency plans for unforeseen issues in each project phase.

Proactively mitigate project challenges and ensure regulatory adherence.

Ensure compliance with local regulations and building codes.

Compliance & Restrictions

Compliance, Safety, or Legal Responsibilities: Must adhere to internal HR policies, labor laws, and regulations related to employee data privacy and fair employment. Must comply with financial controls and anti-corruption standards.

Conflict of Interest Risks: Must maintain impartiality in assessments to avoid bias. Must disclose personal relationships with vendors or partners. Restricted from hiring immediate family without approval.

Confidentiality & Information Security: Handles sensitive business information (strategic plans, financial data, expansion plans). Information security classification: Confidential.

Additional Information

This Job Profile is intended as a reference framework. Actual responsibilities, scope, and requirements may be adjusted based on business priorities and evolving organizational needs.
